trigger: LLM generated a test for a routing function with two required parameters. It passed only the first argument, causing the second (a float score) to default to None — activating the conservative-default branch.
symptom: Test always returns True regardless of input. Method appears to always escalate.
good_code:
# Correct call — all required args:
def test_should_not_escalate():
    history = [{'severity': 0.3}]
    result = router.should_escalate(history, risk_score=0.2)   # explicit score
    assert result == False
detection_command: python3 -c "import inspect; from routing_module import Router; r = Router(); print(inspect.signature(r.should_escalate))"
notes: The test 'passed' on the first run because the conservative default was expected to return False. When the signature was verified, the discrepancy was found.
